title: potentilla concinna var. proxima
intro: Potentilla concinna var. proxima, also known as dwarf five-fingered cinquefoil, is a perennial herb belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Its health benefits include reducing fever, treating infections, and alleviating symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its therapeutic properties.
